keyPoints: Hadoop is a framework for distributed storage and processing of large datasets.
NoSQL databases are used for unstructured or semi-structured data.
Stream processing technologies like Apache Kafka handle real-time data ingestion.
commonMistakes: Assuming Hadoop and Spark are the same thing (Hadoop is for storage/batch, Spark is for in-memory processing).
Thinking all Big Data must be stored in a NoSQL database (relational databases are still important for structured data).
